text: Working with APIs is an essential skill for modern developers. An API allows different software systems to communicate, usually through HTTP requests. The most common methods are GET to retrieve data and POST to send data. Data is often exchanged in JSON format, which is both machine-readable and easy for humans to understand. Many APIs require authentication, typically using an API key, to control access. You should also be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, always implement error handling. APIs may return errors due to invalid requests or server problems, so your code should handle these situations gracefully to avoid crashes.
